task_completion.description: Open Weasis and load the datasets from the specified CT and MR directories.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Weasis application is open and images from the /home/ga/DICOM/studies/crossmod_ct/ and /home/ga/DICOM/studies/crossmod_mr/ directories are loaded into the viewer.

task_completion.description: Perform a simultaneous comparison of the two modalities.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Weasis is configured to show a split-screen or dual viewport layout, displaying images from both datasets side-by-side.

task_completion.description: Adjust window settings, including an attempt at a bone-window assessment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Window/Level controls in Weasis are actively adjusted, or there is a visible change in the contrast/brightness of the images indicating a windowing preset (like 'Bone') was applied.

task_completion.description: Measure a common anatomical structure on both modalities.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Measurement tools (e.g., distance/line tool) are used on the images in both viewports, with numerical measurement values visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Export the annotated views as PNG files to the specified export directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Weasis export/save dialog is used to save images to /home/ga/DICOM/exports/ct_crossmod.png and /home/ga/DICOM/exports/mr_crossmod.png, or a file manager/terminal shows these specific PNG files have been created.

task_completion.description: Save a comparative imaging report containing measurements, percentage difference, and commentary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a report saved to /home/ga/DICOM/exports/crossmodality_report.txt. The text must include numerical measurements, a calculated percentage difference, and comparative commentary on CT vs MRI.

integrity.description: The agent used the Weasis GUI to view, measure, and export the images.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show actual interaction with the Weasis graphical interface, rather than using terminal commands (like ImageMagick) to generate dummy PNG files.

integrity.description: The measurements in the report correspond to actual visual measurements taken in the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numerical values typed into the crossmodality_report.txt match or closely approximate the measurement annotations visible in the Weasis viewports.

integrity.description: The text report contains genuine analysis rather than a copy-paste of the prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report contains specific numerical values and original commentary, not just a verbatim repetition of the task description's requirements.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The datasets provided in the CT and MR directories are actually multi-frame X-Ray Angiography (XA) sequences, not true CT or MR scans. Do not penalize the agent if the images in Weasis look like 2D X-rays/angiograms instead of cross-sectional CT/MRs. The agent should still attempt to fulfill the prompt's instructions (measuring, adjusting windows, and writing a report comparing 'CT' and 'MR').
